name: The Expert Systems Boom
summary: AI's first commercial era. Expert systems — rule-based programs encoding human expertise — proved they could solve real business problems. XCON saved DEC $40M per year configuring computer orders. Japan launched the ambitious Fifth Generation Computer Project. LISP machines became a billion-dollar industry. Corporate investment surged past $1 billion. But the systems were brittle, expensive to maintain, and couldn't learn — setting the stage for the second collapse.
defining_characteristics: AI becomes commercially viable for the first time
Japan's Fifth Generation Project creates international AI competition
LISP machines create a hardware ecosystem around AI
The backpropagation revival (1986) plants seeds for the neural network renaissance
Brittleness of expert systems becomes apparent by the late 1980s
